title: Forest and Conservation Workers
description: Under supervision, perform manual labor necessary to develop, maintain, or protect areas such as forests, forested areas, woodlands, wetlands, and rangelands through such activities as raising and transporting seedlings; combating insects, pests, and diseases harmful to plant life; and building structures to control water, erosion, and leaching of soil. Includes forester aides, seedling pullers, tree planters, and gatherers of nontimber forestry products such as pine straw.

tasks.text: Check equipment to ensure that it is operating properly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fight forest fires or perform prescribed burning tasks under the direction of fire suppression officers or forestry technicians.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform fire protection or suppression duties, such as constructing fire breaks or disposing of brush.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with other workers to discuss issues, such as safety, cutting heights, or work needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain tallies of trees examined and counted during tree marking or measuring efforts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Explain or enforce regulations regarding camping, vehicle use, fires, use of buildings, or sanitation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate skidders, bulldozers, or other prime movers to pull a variety of scarification or site preparation equipment over areas to be regenerated.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Spray or inject vegetation with insecticides to kill insects or to protect against disease or with herbicides to reduce competing vegetation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Thin or space trees, using power thinning saws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify diseased or undesirable trees and remove them, using power saws or hand saws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select or cut trees according to markings or sizes, types, or grades.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prune or shear tree tops or limbs to control growth, increase density, or improve shape.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain campsites or recreational areas, replenishing firewood or other supplies and cleaning kitchens or restrooms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Erect signs or fences, using posthole diggers, shovels, or other hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select tree seedlings, prepare the ground, or plant the trees in reforestation areas, using manual planting tools.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide assistance to forest survey crews by clearing site-lines, holding measuring tools, or setting stakes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sort tree seedlings, discarding substandard seedlings, according to standard charts or verbal instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
